title: How to Create a Gallery Wall: The Best Frames and Layouts
excerpt: Gallery walls look effortlessly curated when done right — and chaotic when done wrong. Here are the frames, tools, and layouts that make the difference.
description: A gallery wall is one of the highest-impact, lowest-cost ways to transform a room. But the execution matters enormously — the wrong frames, mismatched sizing, or poor spacing can make a wall look cluttered instead of curated. We've put together the exact frames and tools that make it look intentional.
metaDescription: The best picture frames and tools for creating a gallery wall in 2025 — layouts, tips, and product picks to make your wall look professionally curated.

products.name: IKEA RIBBA Frame Set (Mixed Sizes)
products.description: The RIBBA is the gallery wall standard for good reason — clean profile, deep mat, and a consistent white finish that makes mixing sizes look intentional. Buy a set and you're halfway done.

products.name: Umbra Trigg Wall Display Shelf
products.description: Adds dimension to a flat gallery wall by letting you lean prints and mix in small objects. The geometric copper finish adds warmth without competing with your art.

products.name: Command Picture Hanging Strips (Value Pack)
products.description: The only way to hang a gallery wall without drilling. Holds up to 16 lbs per pair, removes cleanly without damaging walls, and makes rearranging completely painless. Buy two packs.

products.name: West Elm Thin Metal Gallery Frames
products.description: Thin brushed brass or matte black metal frames that elevate any print to gallery status. The minimal profile lets the art breathe rather than competing with it.
